from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an artist (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Handles a variety of predicates and creates complex
    sentences with relative clauses to connect related facts.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build predicate-to-phrase mapping
    def convert_predicate(subject: str, predicate: str, obj: str, is_relative: bool = False) -> str:
        """Convert a single triple to a natural language phrase."""
        if is_relative:
            # Relative clause format for connecting entities
            mapping = {
                "birthDate": f"was born on {obj}",
                "deathDate": f"died on {obj}",
                "birthPlace": f"was born in {obj}",
                "deathPlace": f"died in {obj}",
                "origin": f"originates from {obj}",
                "nationality": f"is {obj}",
                "genre": f"performs {obj}",
                "instrument": f"plays the {obj}",
                "occupation": f"works as a {obj}",
                "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": f"is associated with {obj}",
                "recordLabel": f"is signed to {obj}",
                "activeYearsStartYear": f"started their career in {obj}",
                "activeYearsEndYear": f"ended their career in {obj}",
                "capital": f"whose capital is {obj}",
                "country": f"in {obj}",
                "language": f"with {obj}",
                "officialLanguage": f"with official language {obj}",
                "parentCompany": f"owned by {obj}",
                "bandMember": f"including {obj}",
                "leader": f"led by {obj}",
                "leaderTitle": f"with leader title {obj}",
                "background": f"a {obj}",
                "alternativeName": f"also known as {obj}",
                "birthYear": f"was born in {obj}",
                "professionalField": f"working in {obj}",
                "musicFusionGenre": f"fusing {obj}",
                "musicSubgenre": f"a subgenre of {obj}",
                "stylisticOrigin": f"originating from {obj}",
                "derivative": f"influencing {obj}",
                "location": f"located in {obj}",
                "governingBody": f"governed by {obj}",
                "ethnicGroup": f"with {obj} ethnic group",
                "distributingCompany": f"distributed by {obj}",
                "training": f"trained at {obj}",
                "populationDensity": f"with population density {obj}",
                "meaning": f"meaning {obj}",
                "longName": f"long name {obj}",
                "areaTotal": f"covering {obj}",
                "foundingDate": f"founded in {obj}",
                "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"at {obj} above sea level",
                "postalCode": f"with postal codes {obj}",
                "isPartOf": f"part of {obj}",
                "demonym": f"residents called {obj}",
                "currency": f"using {obj}",
                "anthem": f"anthem {obj}",
                "cityServed": f"serves the city of {obj}",
            }
            return mapping.get(predicate, f"{predicate} {obj}")
        else:
            # Full sentence format
            mapping = {
                "birthDate": f"{subject} was born on {obj}",
                "deathDate": f"{subject} died on {obj}",
                "birthPlace": f"{subject} was born in {obj}",
                "deathPlace": f"{subject} died in {obj}",
                "origin": f"{subject} originates from {obj}",
                "nationality": f"{subject} is {obj}",
                "genre": f"{subject} performs {obj}",
                "instrument": f"{subject} plays the {obj}",
                "occupation": f"{subject} works as a {obj}",
                "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": f"{subject} is associated with {obj}",
                "recordLabel": f"{subject} is signed to {obj}",
                "activeYearsStartYear": f"{subject} started their career in {obj}",
                "activeYearsEndYear": f"{subject} ended their career in {obj}",
                "capital": f"whose capital is {obj}",
                "country": f"in {obj}",
                "language": f"with {obj}",
                "officialLanguage": f"with official language {obj}",
                "parentCompany": f"owned by {obj}",
                "bandMember": f"including {obj}",
                "leader": f"led by {obj}",
                "leaderTitle": f"with leader title {obj}",
                "background": f"a {obj}",
                "alternativeName": f"also known as {obj}",
                "birthYear": f"{subject} was born in {obj}",
                "professionalField": f"working in {obj}",
                "musicFusionGenre": f"fusing {obj}",
                "musicSubgenre": f"a subgenre of {obj}",
                "stylisticOrigin": f"originating from {obj}",
                "derivative": f"influencing {obj}",
                "location": f"located in {obj}",
                "governingBody": f"governed by {obj}",
                "ethnicGroup": f"with {obj} ethnic group",
                "distributingCompany": f"distributed by {obj}",
                "training": f"trained at {obj}",
                "populationDensity": f"with population density {obj}",
                "meaning": f"meaning {obj}",
                "longName": f"long name {obj}",
                "areaTotal": f"covering {obj}",
                "foundingDate": f"founded in {obj}",
                "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"at {obj} above sea level",
                "postalCode": f"with postal codes {obj}",
                "isPartOf": f"part of {obj}",
                "demonym": f"residents called {obj}",
                "currency": f"using {obj}",
                "anthem": f"anthem {obj}",
                "cityServed": f"serves the city of {obj}",
            }
            return mapping.get(predicate, f"{subject} {predicate} {obj}")

    # Find the main subject (most common subject or first one)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1

    main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get) if subject_counts else ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build main sentence for the main subject
    main_phrases = []
    relative_clauses = []

    for t in subject_triples.get(main_subject, []):
        phrase = convert_predicate(t.subject, t.predicate, t.object)
        # Check if this should be a relative clause (connects to another entity)
        if t.predicate in ["country", "capital", "location", "origin", "birthPlace", "deathPlace", "isPartOf"]:
            relative_clauses.append(phrase)
        else:
            main_phrases.append(phrase)

    # Build the sentence
    if not main_phrases and not relative_clauses:
        return ""

    # Start with main subject info
    if main_phrases:
        main_text = main_phrases[0]
        # Add additional main phrases with commas
        for phrase in main_phrases[1:]:
            main_text += f", {phrase}"
    else:
        main_text = f"{main_subject} " + relative_clauses[0].split(" in ", 1)[-1] if relative_clauses else ""

    # Add relative clauses
    if relative_clauses:
        if main_text:
            main_text += " which is " + relative_clauses[0]
            for clause in relative_clauses[1:]:
                main_text += f", {clause}"
        else:
            main_text = relative_clauses[0]

    return main_text + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
